claim: OpenDataLoader separates standard digital PDFs from hybrid paths for complex tables, scans, OCR, formulas, and chart/image enrichment.
Docling exposes advanced PDF understanding features including layout, reading order, table structure, code, formulas, image classification, and a unified document representation.
Docling treats OCR and table structure as configurable PDF pipeline options before Markdown export.
Marker exposes specialized table and OCR converters in addition to full PDF conversion.
pdf-inspector emphasizes classification, confidence, per-page OCR routing, rectangle/text table detection, encoding detection, and single document load in a Rust deterministic parser.
PyMuPDF4LLM parameterizes Markdown export with OCR, image, page, table, and header/footer options.
A 2026 PDF-to-RAG study found document preparation quality strongly affects downstream QA, with hierarchy-aware splitting and metadata enrichment contributing substantially.
The Docling technical report describes layout analysis and table structure recognition as core conversion capabilities behind an extensible document conversion package.
The local wiki says Markdown rendering should mostly format known structure rather than discover tables, repair reading order, or infer document subtype.
The local wiki prioritizes tagged PDF support, local OCR preprocessing, numeric-heavy table parsing, geometry-aware tables, cross-page table continuity, and debug artifacts.
Structural refactors should map impact surface, change one reversible step at a time, and verify with tests/types/lint.
